programs.admissionRequirements: The DHBW CAS Master’s degree programme is a continuing education and career-integrated programme. The following admission requirements must be met to commence studies: - You have successfully completed a Bachelor's degree (or comparable), regardless of which university, university of applied sciences or cooperative university it is from. - After completing your bachelor's degree, you have worked and gained experience of at least one year in a job suitable for your desired degree programme. You have completed your Bachelor's degree and do not want to waste any time or you don’t yet have a full year of professional experience? Then take advantage of continuing education programme and, if necessary, have your modules credited to a Master's degree course later on. - At work you can implement the knowledge acquired in the Master’s programme. - Your employer is informed about your application for the Master's programme. - The content of the degree course matches the work you do.

programs.title: Autonomous Systems
programs.admissionRequirements: First professionally qualifying university degree (Bachelor, Diplom or equivalent) in a computer science degree course or a mathematical-scientific, engineering or economics degree course with a sufficient computer science component. Sufficient knowledge of concepts, methods and tools in the field of Computer Science. Applicants who have already completed their first degree course in mathematical/scientific subjects or mechanical engineering can be admitted if at least 50% of the computer science content has been taught. In this case, additional courses of up to 30 ECTS may be required. Proof of the required English language skills. No knowledge of the German language is required for this degree course.

programs.title: Computer Science
programs.admissionRequirements: The admission requirements include sufficient knowledge of the concepts, methods and tools for Computer Science. These requirements are usually met through a Computer Science degree with at least 120 ECTS points in Computer Science. For applicants who have obtained a professionally qualifying academic degree - in Computer Science or - in a Mathematical or Natural Sciences subject, - in an Engineering subject or - in an Economics subject admission is possible with at least 90 ECTS points relating to Computer Science, provided that the applicant also gains credits amounting to least 30 credit points, which are determined individually. The grade achieved in the professionally qualifying university degree must be at least 3.0.

programs.admissionRequirements: A professionally qualifying German degree or equivalent degree from a university abroad worth 180 credit points (CP) in Computer Science or another subject with study components in Mathematics modules worth at least 20 CP and Computer Science modules worth at least 10 CP. programs.university: Ludwigshafen University of Business and Society
programs.admissionRequirements: Applicants:who have not completed their first degree in Business Information Systems or Computer Science must show that they have basic knowledge (at least 20 ECTS) of Business Administration disciplines such as Accounting, Investment and Finance, Human Resources, Marketing, Production Theory, Logistics or Corporate Management. Alternatively, the applicant can demonstrate knowledge he or she has acquired through self-learning by taking a written entrance examination on these topics and scoring at least 50% of the maximum points. Successful participation in an examination of knowledge in the areas of Programming, Databases, Web Applications and Business Intelligence is a further requirement. Applicants can prepare for the examination through a free top-up course offered by the department; no prior knowledge is required for this course. Applicants pass the examination if they achieve at least 50% of the points in each of its parts.

Knowledge of (Empirical) Methodology and Social Research (incl. Statistics) must be proved by the end of the application period. programs.title: Computer Science
programs.admissionRequirements: 1. Certificate of academic degree (at minimum six theoretical semesters / 180 ECTS credit points) with a final examination grade of 2.5 or better in Computer Science from a German institution of higher education or an equivalent academic degree from a foreign institution of higher education. Applicants with an over-all pass mark of less than 2.0 must submit proof of the subject aptitude in the context of an aptitude assessment procedure (admission interview). Or: 2. Completed studies (at minimum six theoretical semesters / 180 ECTS credit points), in which Computer Science skills account for a minimum of 40%, with a final examination grade of 2.5 or better from a German institution of higher education or an equivalent academic degree from a foreign institution of higher education. In these instances, subject-related aptitude still needs to be demonstrated as part of the aptitude procedure (admission interview).
programs.englishRequirement: English required (exact score not specified in DAAD summary).
programs.germanRequirement: German institution of higher education or an equivalent academic degree from a foreign institution of higher education

programs.title: Computer Science in Economics
programs.admissionRequirements: The qualification requirement for admission to the degree course is proof of a degree course completed with an overall examination result of 'good' or better 1. in Business Informatics at a German higher education institution or an equivalent academic degree. 2. in a degree course deviating from No. 1, provided that equivalence to this is provided by the subsequent achievement of a maximum of 30 credit points. Insofar as student applicants have to complete subsequent achievements, proof of the missing credit points is a prerequisite for passing the Master's examination. The Examinations Board determines the form in which the credit points are to be made up and informs the student within the first month after enrolment. They must be successfully completed within 12 months of commencing the Master's degree programme.

programs.germanRequirement: German higher education institution or an equivalent academic degree

programs.admissionRequirements: Study places are allocated according to the results of a selection procedure. Prerequisite: - a qualified certificate of academic degree in the subject area of Energy Technology, Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, Electrical and Electronic Engineering or a related engineering or natural science or industrial engineering - generally worth 210 ECTS credits (for applicants with 180 ECTS credits, see the Selection Regulations for DEE) SELECTION CRITERIA: - Average grade of the certificate of academic degree - Subject area of the certificate of academic degree. - Stay abroad of at least one semester (study or practical semester) during the Bachelor's degree. This makes it possible to improve the average grade Admission can be granted on the condition that modules or skills are made up. The necessary knowledge includes the following subjects: Physics (5 ECTS), Electrical Engineering (5 ECTS) Skills in the following subjects are recommended: Computer Science (5 ECTS) + Thermodynamics (5 ECTS)

programs.title: Design
programs.university: Reutlingen University of Applied Sciences for Engineering, Business, Computer Science and Design
programs.admissionRequirements: The admission requirement is a qualifying degree in Art or Design and evidence of artistic aptitude by submission of a portfolio. - Compilation and presentation of previous creative work, preferably the final assignment and work from work placements or industrial employment. - A letter of motivation with a short concept (max. 2 DIN A4 pages) for a possible semester project in the desired specialisation. The portfolio and the letter of motivation with the short concept should show the different aspects of the previous creative work and the objectives of the applicant. The short concept serves to assess the applicants. Portfolio size between A4 and A3. In exceptional cases with larger artistic works, the portfolio size 80 cm x 100 cm. Larger folders, rolls and bulky containers are NOT accepted. It is not possible to submit the artistic work in digital form.

programs.admissionRequirements: The admission requirements for the degree course are: 1. a completed degree worth at least 210 ECTS in the field of Economics with a minimum grade of 2.5 and 2.3 for the Canadian partner). Graduates who have completed a six-semester degree course worth 180 ECTS can be admitted and must also complete a compulsory work placement integrated into the degree course of 900 hours (approx. 20-24 weeks) at the end of the 2nd stage of studies. For this compulsory work placement, the faculty of the ESB Business School awards 30 ECTS. Applicants with a completed degree in another subject area must provide proof of at least 4 Economics modules. Of these, at least 2 must be in the quantitative field (e.g. Accounting, Financial Management, Statistics, Economics, Logistics), 2. very good proficiency in English and 3. at least six months of practical experience before commencing the Master's course.

programs.admissionRequirements: Admission to the Master's degree course in Artificial Intelligence can be granted to those who have very good or good knowledge and skills in the field of computer science and/or mathematics, demonstrated by a relevant, practice-oriented, scientifically based qualification. programs.admissionRequirements: Admission to the Master's degree course requires proof of a first professionally qualifying certificate of academic degree with above average grade in Computer Science or a related field at the Rosenheim University of Applied Sciences; or through a recognised equivalent qualification from a German or foreign university. Applicants for the Master's degree course must, in addition to the above mentioned admission requirements, prove their ability and aptitude by passing an aptitude test. Other especially suited applicants may take the aptitude test, if they can be granted admission to a Master's degree in Computer Science at another German university. An admission requirement for the Master's degree also is sufficient knowledge of both written and spoken English at least equivalent to those demonstrated by a bachelor's degree in Computer Science from the Rosenheim University of Applied Sciences.

programs.title: Data Science
programs.admissionRequirements: Admission is based on an aptitude procedure, which is laid down in Section 4 and Annex I of the examination regulations. This requires, among other things, an average grade of 2.70 or better in the previous BSc degree programme. Furthermore, proof of the following knowledge from the Bachelor’s degree is expected: - Multidimensional Analysis (at least 5 CP ECTS) - Abstract Structures of (Linear) Algebra (at least 5 CP ECTS) - Basics of an Advanced Programming Language (at least 4 CP ECTS) - Practical Programming (at least 4 CP ECTS) - Algorithms or Numerical Procedures (at least 5 CP ECTS) - Discrete Structures, Databases or Data Engineering (at least 5 CP ECTS) -Data Science or Machine Learning (at least 4 CP ECTS)
programs.englishRequirement: English required (exact score not specified in DAAD summary).

programs.title: Geo-informatics
programs.admissionRequirements: 1. Academic degree of a Bachelor’s course in Geoinformatics or Geography with Geoinformatics (at least 20 ECTS) or Computer Science with Geography as a minor/application subject (at least 12 ECTS) or subject-related courses at a German university. 2. Overall grade of at least 2.99 according to the General Examination Regulations of the University of Augsburg or another equivalent German or foreign academic degree in natural sciences with an equivalent overall grade. 3. Basic knowledge in a programming or script language as well as a minimum Level C1 English. 4. Subject-specific proof: at a minimum, you must have taken a lecture on geoinformatics, a lecture on cartography, a tutorial on geoinformation systems, an application course involving GIS or programming, and a programming course (language irrelevant). Knowledge of GIS is mandatory.
programs.englishRequirement: English

programs.admissionRequirements: A subject related requirement is proof of a degree in the field of bioinformatics completed with good academic results. In the case of a degree being held in another subject (e.g. Computer Science, Biology), the examination of professional aptitude can, if necessary, be carried out by means of an admission interview. programs.title: Business computing
programs.admissionRequirements: A subject related requirement is a degree course in the field of Economic Sciences, over which course achievements must be proven to the following extent: 40 credit points in Business Administration, 5 credit points in Mathematics, 5 credit points in Statistics and 18 credit points in Business Informatics. In a first information technology degree course, proof of study achievements must be provided as follows: 60 credit points in practical Computer Science and 12 credit points in Economics. With the Bachelor's degree,graduates should have a grade of 2.5 or better. Likewise, English language skills at level B2 (according to the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR)) must be demonstrated. International applicants must provide proof of German language proficiency at the level of the DSH examination (min. level 2), the TestDaF (TDN 4x4) or a recognised equivalent.

programs.title: Computational and Data Science
programs.admissionRequirements: A subject related requirement is proof of a degree in the field of engineering or natural sciences, in particular computer science, mathematics, bioinformatics, business informatics or business mathematics. At least 21 credit points in linear algebra, analysis, stochastics and numerics and at least 18 credit points in programming, algorithms and data structures are required. In principle, an interest in interdisciplinary contexts and a corresponding degree of prior interdisciplinary knowledge in computer science, mathematics, natural sciences or engineering is expected. A very good knowledge of English is also indispensable for the course. International applicants must provide proof of German language proficiency at the level of the DSH examination (min. level 2), the TestDaF (TDN 4x4) or a recognised equivalent.

programs.title: Digital Engineering
programs.admissionRequirements: The admission requirement is evidence of a good or very good Bachelor's degree (or equivalent) in Computer Science or in a discipline related to Computer Science or in an Engineering discipline with a standard period of study of at least 3 years. Furthermore, basic knowledge in computer science must be demonstrated through: - at least 20 CP (ECTS) in Computer Science courses (incl. projects in the core area of Digital Engineering) or - at least one year of relevant professional experience in Computer Science-related activities, where one year per 10 CP (ECTS) can compensate for a shortfall in Computer Science courses. In addition, evidence must be provided of a sufficient proficiency in English or German at the level of the higher education entrance qualification. International applicants must have passed the "German Language Test for Admission to Higher Education for Foreign Students" (DSH-2) or the "Test of German as a Foreign Language" (TestDaF) with 4/4/4/4 or the TOEFL or the IELTS.
